industries.name: Glass Decolorizing & Colorizing
uses: Selenium decolorizes glass by neutralizing the green tint caused by iron impurities (Fe²⁺) — selenium and iron together produce a complementary color balance, used in clear container glass and float glass production

Cadmium sulfoselenide (CdSₓSe₁₋ₓ) produces brilliant red, orange, and yellow pigments in glass, ceramics, and high-temperature plastics — selenium content controls hue from yellow (low Se) to deep red (high Se)
Bronze-tinted architectural glass: selenium with iron produces the warm brown tint in solar control glazing for commercial building facades and automobile windshields
Ruby selenium glass for signal lenses: traffic lights, railway signals, and marine lights historically used selenium ruby glass for its saturated red color and heat resistance

companies.name: Umicore
companies.use: World's largest refined selenium producer. Recovers selenium from copper anode slimes generated during electrolytic copper refining. Supplies selenium to glass, pigment, semiconductor, and agricultural chemical industries.

companies.name: AGC Inc. (Asahi Glass)
companies.use: Major flat glass producer using selenium for decolorizing container and float glass, and for producing selenium-containing solar control and tinted architectural glazing products.

industries.name: Thin-Film Solar Cells
uses: CIGS (copper indium gallium selenide, CuIn₁₋ₓGaₓSe₂) thin-film solar cells: selenium forms the p-type absorber layer in CIGS panels achieving 22–23% cell efficiency — competitive with silicon but deposited as thin film on flexible or rigid substrates
CIGS modules offer advantages over silicon for building-integrated PV (BIPV), flexible rooftop applications, and low-light performance; selenium is typically 20–25% of CIGS absorber layer by weight
CdTe (cadmium telluride) thin-film cells — the dominant non-silicon technology (First Solar) — sometimes use a thin selenium layer at the back contact interface for passivation
Perovskite solar cells under development use selenium-based hole transport layers and selenium passivation of grain boundaries to improve stability and efficiency

companies.name: Solar Frontier (Showa Shell/Idemitsu)
companies.use: World's largest CIGS thin-film solar module manufacturer. Produces CIS/CIGS panels at Kunitomi factory in Miyazaki — selenium is a key absorber material in its copper indium selenide solar cell technology.

companies.name: Avancis (CNBM)
companies.use: European CIGS thin-film solar module manufacturer producing building-integrated PV panels. Uses selenium as a primary absorber layer component in its CIGS deposition process at its Dresden manufacturing facility.

industries.name: Electronics, Xerography & Chemicals
uses: Amorphous selenium (a-Se) as the X-ray photoconductor in direct digital flat-panel X-ray detectors for mammography and chest radiography — selenium converts X-ray photons directly to electron-hole pairs with very high spatial resolution
Selenium rectifiers and photocells: historically the dominant solid-state rectifier before silicon diodes; selenium photocells used in light meters and automatic exposure control in cameras
Selenium dioxide (SeO₂) as oxidizing agent in organic synthesis and as a reagent for allylic and benzylic oxidations in pharmaceutical and fine chemical manufacturing
Selenium sulfide (SeS₂) as active ingredient in anti-dandruff shampoos (Selsun Blue) — disrupts Malassezia yeast growth on the scalp that causes dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis

Selenium as dietary supplement and animal feed additive: essential micronutrient (as selenocysteine in selenoproteins) for thyroid hormone metabolism and antioxidant defense — deficiency causes Keshan disease

companies.name: Agfa-Gevaert
companies.share: ~40% of selenium flat-panel X-ray detector market
companies.use: Leading manufacturer of amorphous selenium (a-Se) flat-panel digital X-ray detectors for direct radiography and mammography. Its DX-D direct conversion detectors use thick a-Se layers for high-sensitivity X-ray imaging.

companies.name: Iofina
companies.use: Specialty iodine and selenium chemicals producer. Produces selenium compounds for pharmaceutical, electronic, and agricultural applications from natural brine selenium sources in Oklahoma.

industries.name: Agriculture & Nutrition
uses: Selenium fertilizers (sodium selenate, Na₂SeO₄) applied to selenium-deficient soils — Finland mandatorily adds selenium to all nitrogen fertilizers since 1984 to address nationwide Se deficiency in crops and livestock

Selenium premixes in animal feed: prevents white muscle disease in sheep and cattle, exudative diathesis in poultry, and hepatosis dietetica in pigs — essential trace element for animal health at 0.1–0.3 ppm in diet

Selenomethionine and sodium selenite in human dietary supplements — selenium is an essential micronutrient with a narrow therapeutic window; deficiency linked to thyroid disorders and compromised immune function

Biofortification of staple crops with selenium: agronomic selenium application to wheat and rice improves human selenium status in populations subsisting on crops from selenium-poor soils (large areas of China, New Zealand, and central Europe)

companies.name: Alltech
companies.use: Major animal nutrition company producing Sel-Plex organic selenium yeast supplement for livestock and poultry feed. Organic selenium (selenomethionine) is more bioavailable than inorganic sodium selenite in animal nutrition.

companies.name: Yara International
companies.use: Major fertilizer company adding selenium to fertilizers in Finland and other selenium-deficient markets. Supplies selenium-enriched nitrogen fertilizers that have dramatically improved population selenium status in Finland since the 1980s.
